name: llada_8b_instruct_K128
description: LLaDA-8B-Instruct diffusion language model with K=128 denoising iterations at d_model=4096, 8-bit audit discretization (Q=256). Only the final denoised output is logged; all K−1 intermediate denoising states are unlogged. The min-cut selects a single denoising edge, giving ε_state^UB = d · log₂(Q) = 4096 · 8 = 32768 bits, independent of K.
notes: LLaDA-8B-Instruct uses iterative unmasking: at each denoising step the model predicts all tokens simultaneously from the current partially-masked sequence. Intermediate denoising steps 3..K−2 are elided; the DAG is a chain s_init → s_1 → s_2 → ... → s_{K-1} → S_t.

Per-edge capacity: c_e = d · log₂(Q) = 4096 · 8 = 32768 bits, where Q=256 (8-bit audit discretization). This is an auditor-side discretization choice reflecting a uniform quantization grid over the latent state; it is not a claim about the model's internal bf16 precision.

The cut-set capacity is defined over channel interfaces in the unrolled deployment graph; repeated iterative reuse of the same bounded latent interface does not increase the worst-case one-step information bottleneck. The min-cut selects a single edge in the denoising chain, giving ε_UB = 32768 bits independent of K.
The full chain sum is Σ c_e = K · 32768 bits (a loose upper bound; Corollary 4.4 min-cut is tighter).
Architecture config: d_model=4096, n_layers=32, n_heads=32, vocab_size=126464, max_sequence_length=4096 (from LLaDA-8B-Instruct config.json).
